message:
fix(typography): restore full interactive-article scoped style system after globals regression

Emergency hotfix for a major readability regression introduced by the recent `app/globals.css` consolidation.

Root cause:
- The previous global CSS change removed large scoped blocks that define article-specific typography and visual treatment, especially for `.overprompting-scope`, and also impacted other scoped article systems.
- As a result, article pages fell back to generic/default styles, collapsing heading/body hierarchy and making multiple sections appear tiny or visually broken.

What this commit does:
- Restores `app/globals.css` article-scoped style coverage to the known-good baseline from commit `62a3567` (the last state where those article pages rendered correctly).
- Reapplies the targeted CMA-ES hero title sizing adjustment requested afterward:
  - `.cmaes-scope .rq-display-title`
  - `font-size: clamp(3rem, 8.5vw, 7rem)`
  - `line-height: 0.9`
  - `letter-spacing: -0.045em`
- Preserves interactive-article selector coverage for all major scoped pages:
  - `.overprompting-scope`
  - `.hoeffding-scope`
  - `.raptorq-scope`
  - `.bakery-scope`
  - `.cmaes-scope`
  - `.barra-scope`
- Includes Beads issue state update in `.beads/issues.jsonl` for this emergency bugfix.

Validation:
- Type-check: `bun tsc --noEmit` passes.
- Lint: `bun lint` passes with warnings only (no errors).

Outcome:
- Restores intended typography hierarchy and scoped visual language on interactive writing pages, including `/writing/overprompting`.
